title: Golden Firmware Image Compliance Across NDFC Fabrics
spl:
index=cisco_dc sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory" earliest=-24h
| lookup ndfc_golden_image.csv model OUTPUT golden_version
| where isnotnull(golden_version) AND software_version!=golden_version
| stats values(software_version) as running_versions values(golden_version) as golden_versions count by fabric_name, model
| sort -count
| table fabric_name, model, running_versions, golden_versions, count
description: Running multiple NX-OS trains in one fabric increases interoperability defects during upgrades. Comparing live images to the approved golden list per platform reduces unplanned reload risk and speeds security patch campaigns for the physical data center network.
value: Running multiple NX-OS trains in one fabric increases interoperability defects during upgrades. Comparing live images to the approved golden list per platform reduces unplanned reload risk and speeds security patch campaigns for the physical data center network.
implementation: (1) Maintain `ndfc_golden_image.csv` with CAB-approved NX-OS per SKU. (2) Nightly diff from inventory API. (3) Drive remediation tickets with risk tier from PSIRT correlation (UC-18.4.3).
visualization: Pie chart (compliant vs drift), Table (non-compliant switches), Bar chart (count by fabric).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), `cisco:ndfc:inventory`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_dc` `sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory"` with fields `switch_name`, `fabric_name`, `model`, `software_version`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `ndfc_golden_image.csv` with CAB-approved NX-OS per SKU. (2) Nightly diff from inventory API. (3) Drive remediation tickets with risk tier from PSIRT correlation (UC-18.4.3).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_dc sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory" earliest=-24h
| lookup ndfc_golden_image.csv model OUTPUT golden_version
| where isnotnull(golden_version) AND software_version!=golden_version
| stats values(software_version) as running_versions values(golden_version) as golden_versions count by fabric_name, model
| sort -count
| table fabric_name, model, running_versions, golden_versions, count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Golden Firmware Image Compliance Across NDFC Fabrics** ‚Äî Running multiple NX-OS trains in one fabric increases interoperability defects during upgrades. Comparing live images to the approved golden list per platform reduces unplanned reload risk and speeds security patch campaigns for the physical data center network.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_dc` `sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory"` with fields `switch_name`, `fabric_name`, `model`, `software_version`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cisco DC Networking Application (Splunkbase 7777), `cisco:ndfc:inventory`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_dc; **sourcetype**: cisco:ndfc:inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cisco_dc, sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:inventory", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(golden_version) AND software_version!=golden_version` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by fabric_name, model** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Golden Firmware Image Compliance Across NDFC Fabrics**): table fabric_name, model, running_versions, golden_versions, count


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie chart (compliant vs drift), Table (non-compliant switches), Bar chart (count by fabric).
grandmaExplanation: Running multiple NX-OS trains in one fabric increases interoperability defects during upgrades ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
